summary: criticOutput test helper missing `verdict` field causes all happy-path tests to fail
detail: In pg-lifecycle-proof.test.ts:88-99, criticOutput(verdict) constructs a RoleOutput but omits the `verdict` field. The RoleOutput type for critic at secretary.ts:63 requires `readonly verdict: "approved" | "rejected"`. Since verdict is undefined, roleOutputToEvent at secretary.ts:79 produces CriticComplete with undefined verdict, which toSMEvent at line 204 interprets as "rejected" (undefined !== "approved"), routing the state machine back to planning instead of forward to approved. This breaks 12 of 16 tests. Meanwhile, criticOutput("rejected") works by accident because undefined verdict produces the same outcome.
